---
title: "Lecture 15 Notes: Perception III - Modern Robot Perception"
---

## Lecture slide

- [Lecture 15 slides](../slides/15-real-world-robot-control.qmd)

## Key ideas

- Modern robot perception combines geometric methods with learned representations.
- Multimodal inputs (RGB, depth, language, force) improve task-level understanding.
- Robustness to domain shift and uncertainty is essential for deployment.

## Common workflow

1. Build scene representation from multimodal sensor streams.
2. Run detection/segmentation/tracking with learned or hybrid models.
3. Estimate uncertainty and hand off structured outputs to planning/control.

## Review questions

1. What benefits does multimodal sensing provide over RGB-only perception?
2. Why is uncertainty estimation important in robot decision pipelines?
3. How can domain adaptation reduce performance drop in new environments?
